summaries.title: SUPERROO_VAULT_KEY missing from PM2 env - readEnvValue() fallback fix
summaries.summary: The bug occurred because `SUPERROO_VAULT_KEY` was set in the `.env` file but not read by the PM2 process, as the ecosystem config only used `process.env.SUPERROO_VAULT_KEY || ""` (which reads from the shell environment, not `.env`). The fix added `readEnvValue("SUPERROO_VAULT_KEY")` as a fallback and required `pm2 delete` then `pm2 start` (not `pm2 restart`) to force PM2 to re-evaluate the config. Key takeaway: Always use `readEnvValue()` fallback for `.env` variables in PM2 configs, and use `pm2 delete` + `pm2 start` to apply changes.
